response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r4c0 is in acute crisis: islanded with SoC of 0.35/3 kWh (11.7%), supporting a 1.00 kW load, with only ~21 minutes until depletion. The memory log reveals a consistent pattern of rejections from neighboring peers (r3c0, r4c1) who cite their own critical thresholds and network-wide cascading stress. All rejections recommend requesting from r2c0, identified as the most reliable donor. With no established trust circles and geographic neighbors equally or more constrained, r4c0 must immediately pivot to inbound request mode targeting r2c0 with urgent priority, while simultaneously preparing for load shedding as a fallback. The microgrid appears to be under systemic stress with multiple households approaching depletion; r4c0 cannot afford to share outbound and must focus entirely on self-preservation through emergency inbound acquisition or controlled load reduction.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "r4c0 SoC critically low (11.7%); depletion horizon ~21 min. Islanded microgrid under cascading stress. All peers (r3c0, r4c1) rejected sharing due to own critical thresholds. r2c0 identified as most reliable donor. No outbound sharing possible without risking immediate blackout. Recommend urgent inbound request to r2c0 and load shedding contingency."
ttl_ticks: 3
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
